nodes.name: Get TMetric Users for user mapping
Merge user info with time entries

parameters.content:
## Step 1:  Collecting the data from Tmetric and process it
In this step we use custom API calls to Tmetric API to collect are necessary data. Then we match it to the correct user, assign it to make easier to use later.

For Work Schedule we get data from beginning of the month up until now, so we can calculate missing time correctly
For Projects Data we fetch entire month so we know how much of the budget was spent

In the end we use set to create cohesieve repository that will be easy to work with

If you want to learn more about Tmetric API check their docs [here](https://app.tmetric.com/api-docs/)

## Set up Globals and check if workflow runs from schedule or click
Because we specify in "Globals" data that is required also in "Setup" we must check what invoked "If" node. Luckily, it's very easy, because "Run Once a Week" trigger returns data which we can check against in our "If" node

## Step 2: Process each user, fetch more user specific data, and generate data for personalized feedback
In this step we have to go back to Tmetric API. Using previously obtained User ID, we are obtaining users Time Entries without currently running tasks that are later used to calculate how much time they in fact spent working. 
We also use Work Schedule and Time Off Requests to see how much time user should actually have spent. Then, we convert it to percentages to see if user met our criteria

We use wait node to take a short break between successive calls so we don't hit rate limits.

## Step 3: Send raport
In this step we use N8N Data Table we created previously and look for users Slack ID so we can send them message directly.

Message is built using Slack Block Builder Kit available under [this link](https://app.slack.com/block-kit-builder/). With it's help we can create styled messages that are not boring.

## Run once before you run on schedule

This workflow requires N8N Data Table in order to work. Once you run "Setup" workflow will check if required Data Table already exists, if no, it will create it.
Then, it will make API Call to Tmetric, retrieve users data, retrieve Slack users and filter out accounts that are invalid.

Then it will send a message to the channel, pinging specific users to fill out the form where they are requested to select their Tmetric account.

This is needed in order to be able to send later personalized private messages directly to the users

### Run again

If new user has been added to Tmetric or Slack, or somebody made mistake, you can safely run this step again. It won't recreate the table but send a form again to all users

### Important

If user shouldn't be notified they should just submit the form without selecting any value

## TMetric to Slack employees time manager
### How it works
This workflow checks weekly if users meet their hourly quota at the end of trhe week.
It will help them keep track of how much they worked, on what projects, remind them if they forgot to put entries in the system

It leverages Tmetric API in order to obtain data, Slack, and N8N Data Tables

#### Create tables
1. We check if data table with given name already exists, if yes, we skip to the next part, if not, we create it
2. We get Tmetric and Slack users, filter out users that are bots or inactive
3. We sen

#### Run Once a Week
1. We colect data from TMetric using custom HTTP Requests, calculate percentage of project budget spent, getting users, their Work Schedules and Time Off Requests
2. We process each user individually, get their Time Entries, merge all data together into one cohesieve piece
3. We get users Slack id from N8N Data table we created during set up using their Tmetric id to send personalized message directly to them

###Setup
In order for workflow to work you will need to setup couple variables. Most likely you will be only interested in just 2 first, but you can also set name of the Data Table to prevent any problems

- Fill out "Globals" node
  - tmAccountId - You can find it in URL once you visit Tmetric, in this example it's all 0's: https://app.tmetric.com/#/tracker/000000/
  - Allow hours missing percentage - If users have more percentages of missing time spent on working they will get information about it
  - tmetricToSlackUserDataTableName - Name of the data table used across the project 

- Auth
In order for this workflow to work you have to set up auth for HTTP Request Nodes. In order to do this, open one of these nodes and select:
Authentication > General Credential Type > Header Auth 
And if you doesn't already have Tmetric credentials Create new of following shape:

Name:
Authorization
Value:
<Your Tmetric API Key>

You can learn how to get API key [here](https://app.tmetric.com/api-docs/)

### Customize
If you want to customize message that is sent on Slack edit "Send a message" node. You can also change there if message should be sent directly or to the channel





Need help? Contact us at developers@sailingbyte.com or at sailingbyte.com

Happy hacking!
